target.title: fix(auto-reply): fast path text control commands
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a strict fast path for whole-message text control commands such as `/status`, `/thinking`, `/id`, `/usage`, `/send`, and `/activation`.
- Mark Feishu/Lark whole-message text slash commands as structured text command turns so the core fast path sees the actual channel context shape.
- Reuse the existing status, directive, and local command handlers so these replies can return before workspace/bootstrap or Codex runtime execution.
- Keep risky or prompt-bearing commands on the normal path, including `/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/model`, `/queue`, `/stop`, `/exec`, `/trace`, inline command tokens, and commands with trailing prompt text.

## Root Cause / Impact
Feishu/Lark plain-text slash commands are not native slash commands. They can arrive as authorized text command messages, and Codex/GPT-backed sessions may otherwise route those local controls through the slower normal agent path. That can make `/status` or `/thinking` slow or fail to produce a visible operational reply even though they should be handled locally.

## Review Follow-up
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the approval SDK/channel changes from this PR scope; the diff is now limited to Feishu text-command context, the core safe fast path, focused tests, and the script lint fix.
- Feishu/Lark now attach a structured `CommandTurn` with `kind: "text-slash"` for whole-message text slash commands.
- The core fast path now consumes structured text command turns and prefers `CommandTurn.body`, which covers normalized Feishu/Lark command bodies such as mention-stripped group commands.
- Added regression coverage for the actual Feishu-built context shape and for structured text command turns without relying only on legacy `CommandSource: "text"`.
- Rebasing follow-up: current PR head at validation time is `cc8588437060`, rebased on `upstream/main` at `7a36bb37afaf`; GitHub reports the PR as mergeable with no repository merge conflict.
- CI follow-up: `check-guards` repeatedly OOMed in the plugin SDK declaration smoke at the default ~2GB V8 heap, so this PR now gives the check shard the same `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` budget already used by other memory-heavy CI paths.

## Related Issues
- Related to #82356, the canonical Feishu/Lark Codex/GPT slash-command `delivered=false` report. This PR addresses the low-risk local control-command subset by returning those replies before runtime execution.
- Related to #84155, which was closed as a duplicate of #82356 and contains newer gpt-5.5 Feishu command no-response logs.
- Adjacent to #78689, but this PR does not claim to fix the broader native Codex Feishu direct-session/message-tool HTTP 400 delivery evidence.
- Adjacent to #79409, but this PR intentionally excludes stateful/destructive/complex commands such as `/compact`, `/new`, and `/stop`, so it does not close that broader authorization/command-handling issue.

## Safety
- Requires the normal text-command enablement check.
- Requires authorized senders before returning local command replies.
- Only handles single-line, whole-message commands from a small whitelist.
- Does not mark inline command tokens such as `please check /status` as text command turns.
- Falls back to the existing reply flow when any prompt text remains.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/command-turn-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- Extra WhatsApp regression attempt: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts` partially ran; `inbound-dispatch.test.ts` passed, but `process-message.test.ts` could not load locally because `@protobufjs/aspromise` was missing from the interrupted local install.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` was attempted but did not complete locally because `pnpm exec`/hook dependency status checks repeatedly stalled on registry downloads for optional platform packages.

Current-head validation after rebase to `upstream/main` at `7a36bb37afaf` and PR head `cc8588437060`:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts` (22 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts` (77 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/check-memory-fd-repro.test.ts` (7 tests)
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/check-memory-fd-repro.mjs --format=github`
- `git diff --check`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- GitHub `Real behavior proof` check passes on the current head.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu/Lark whole-message text control commands such as `/status` should return a local operational reply instead of being routed through the slow Codex/GPT agent path and completing with no visible reply.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway started from this PR source at original proof head `c81dbf2f13c4`, OpenClaw `2026.5.22`, with the Feishu plugin loaded from `/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions/feishu/index.ts`. The runtime used a real Feishu `feishu2` WebSocket account and a real p2p Feishu DM. Secrets and user/chat IDs are redacted below. After later review follow-up, the approval SDK/channel changes were removed and the current validation head `cc8588437060` was rebased onto `upstream/main` at `7a36bb37afaf`; the same Feishu fast-path behavior remains covered by focused current-head tests and the GitHub Real behavior proof check.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a Feishu-only proof gateway from the PR source:

```sh
NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr85616-feishu2-minimal.json \
OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions \
OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 \
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_STARTUP_MODEL_PREWARM=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force --ws-log compact --verbose
```

Then sent `/status` from the real Feishu `feishu2` DM.

Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log excerpt from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-23.log`:

```text
2026-05-23T21:49:35.639+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: feishu; 29.9s)
2026-05-23T21:49:39.831+08:00 [feishu] starting feishu[feishu2] (mode: websocket)
2026-05-23T21:49:56.165+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: WebSocket client started
2026-05-23T21:49:58.334+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
2026-05-23T21:52:22.137+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: received message from <redacted-open-id> in <redacted-chat-id> (p2p)
2026-05-23T21:52:22.150+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T21:52:25.438+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
```

For comparison, before this patch on installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20`, the same real Feishu2 `/status` path reached dispatch but produced no visible reply:

```text
2026-05-23T19:45:43.331+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T19:45:44.119+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)
```

Observed result after fix: The real Feishu2 `/status` DM now exits through the local control-command fast path and produces one queued final reply (`queuedFinal=true, replies=1`) instead of the previous no-reply completion (`queuedFinal=false, replies=0`).

What was not tested: This runtime proof covers the safe local text-control subset via Feishu p2p DM. It does not claim coverage for destructive/stateful commands (`/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/stop`), prompt-bearing slash commands, native slash-command payloads, or group mention routing.

target.title: Control-plane hardening from Optimus ops findings
target.body:
## Summary

Completes the OpenClaw control-plane hardening project stack from Optimus ops findings:

- ADP-237: structured A2A handoff IDs and return-route tracing.
- ADP-238: session/status diagnostics and safer control-plane visibility.
- ADP-239: channel/runtime boundary checks.
- ADP-240: plugin contract validation surfaces.
- ADP-241: plugin contract validator CLI path.
- ADP-242: non-destructive doctor/probe guardrails.
- ADP-243: unified `openclaw diagnose --json` control-plane API.

Latest merged head `8c5e45715370bdd2810b5a0813ee98e6fe4c02bc` adds the maintainer-facing diagnose contract cleanup:

- Documents the operator JSON contract in `docs/diagnostics/diagnose-json-contract.md`.
- Adds `schemaVersion: "openclaw-diagnose/v1"` to the diagnose payload.
- Adds explicit redaction guarantees: no secrets, raw config, or raw env included.
- Adds explicit persistence declarations for baseline, probe-cache, and incident-ledger writes.
- Adds focused tests for diagnose schema/redaction/persistence and gateway auth omission.
- Merges current `origin/main` and resolves the configured-state discovery conflict.

## Maintainer Contract Checklist

Please explicitly review/accept these operator-facing contracts before merge:

- `diagnose --json` payload schema version: `openclaw-diagnose/v1`.
- Persistent state behavior: latest baseline snapshot, probe-cache refresh, and summarized incident-ledger entry on failed checks.
- Redaction boundary: no raw gateway auth, raw config, raw env, token, password, API key, or URL userinfo material in emitted JSON.
- Non-destructive behavior: unsafe actions are recommendation strings only; this path must not run doctor repair, gateway restart, plugin update, credential rotation, or live channel startup.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The latest cleanup makes the `openclaw diagnose --json` operator contract explicit and reviewable by adding a schema version, redaction guarantees, persistence declarations, docs, and tests. The branch also remains current with upstream `main` after resolving the configured-state discovery merge conflict.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw development checkout in a clean temporary worktree at `/tmp/openclaw-pr85583`, merged with `origin/main` and checked at head `8c5e45715370bdd2810b5a0813ee98e6fe4c02bc`. No destructive doctor repairs, service restarts, live plugin updates, production channel startup, credential rotation, AWS/prod actions, or secret reads were run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/probes/cache.test.ts src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/incidents/ledger.test.ts src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts`
  2. `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
  3. `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
  4. `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/probes/cache.test.ts src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/incidents/ledger.test.ts src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 20.88s
```

```text
$ CI=true pnpm lint:core
[oxlint:core:src:...] finished
[oxlint:core:ui] finished
[oxlint:core:packages] finished
```

```text
$ CI=true pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```

```text
$ git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: `buildDiagnoseJson()` now emits `schemaVersion: "openclaw-diagnose/v1"`, explicit redaction guarantees, and explicit persistence declarations while preserving the existing baseline/probe-cache/incident/task/plugin contract. The focused tests confirm diagnose writes the expected baseline/probe evidence, threads timeout into baseline gateway probes, omits `gateway.auth`, avoids raw token/password/API-key material in the emitted JSON, and keeps unsafe actions as recommendation strings. The configured-state merge conflict resolves to the upstream discovery-aware function signature while preserving sorted channel ID behavior in the package-state helper.
- What was not tested: I did not run destructive doctor repairs, service restarts, live plugin updates, production channel startup, credential rotation, live `diagnose` against user channels, AWS/prod infrastructure, database access, or secret reads. Those are intentionally outside this non-destructive PR validation path.

Prior full proof from earlier branch head remains relevant for the broader control-plane hardening changes:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/probes/cache.test.ts src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 15.40s
```

```text
$ pnpm build
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.
```

```json
$ node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "strict": true,
  "pluginCount": 125,
  "findingCount": 30
}
```

```json
$ timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000
{
  "ok": true,
  "baselineMetrics": {
    "sessionCount": 30,
    "agentCount": 0,
    "channelCount": 1,
    "activeTaskCount": 0
  },
  "baselineComponents": {
    "gateway": "pass",
    "channels": "pass",
    "agents": "pass",
    "tasks": "pass",
    "locks": "pass",
    "plugins": "pass"
  },
  "gatewayMessage": "Gateway responded",
  "channelMessage": "All 1 channels connected",
  "taskMessage": "0 active tasks",
  "pluginsMessage": "125 plugins loaded",
  "probeCache": {
    "count": 1,
    "stale": 0
  },
  "incidents": {
    "count": 0,
    "open": 0,
    "frozen": 0,
    "repairs": 0
  }
}
```

## Validation

Latest cleanup on merged head:

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/probes/cache.test.ts src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/incidents/ledger.test.ts src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

Earlier full branch validation:

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/probes/cache.test.ts src/baseline/capture.test.ts src/commands/diagnose.test.ts src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm build`
- `node dist/entry.js plugins contracts validate --strict --json`
- `timeout 30s node dist/entry.js diagnose --json --timeout 1000`

## Notes

The upstream repository is creator/maintainer-owned, so I did not bypass review. This update is limited to the PR branch, operator-contract cleanup, proof refresh, and maintainer review request.

The PR passes a fixed Slack `loading_messages` array into native assistant thread-status calls, changes the initial Slack status copy, updates Slack status tests, and edits `CHANGELOG.md`.

target.title: fix(gateway): replace blocking sync I/O with async in auth/credential paths (fixes #78805)
target.body:
## Problem
Gateway auth and credential resolution paths perform synchronous I/O (keychain reads, credential-file reads, config-command resolution) that block the Node.js event loop, causing Gateway/Telegram stalls under load.

Fixes #78805

## Fix
Add async credential and config-resolution helpers (`readKeychainValueAsync`, `readCredentialFileAsync`, `resolveConfigValueAsync`, etc.) and switch selected CLI auth epoch and auth-storage calls to await the async variants.

## Test plan
1. Verify existing credential and auth tests still pass
2. Verify no regressions in auth-storage, cli-credentials, cli-auth-epoch
3. Verify config resolution continues to work correctly

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
✓ src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts
✓ src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts
✓ src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
   Duration  5.72s
```

All 37 credential and auth storage tests pass, confirming no regressions from the async migration of auth/credential/config paths.

target.title: Check ClawHub trust before plugin and skill installs
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw now checks ClawHub exact-release trust before downloading ClawHub plugin releases and archive-backed ClawHub skill releases. Malicious or ClawHub-blocked releases fail closed before download; suspicious or failed-review releases require an interactive acknowledgement or `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk`; pending or stale non-risk trust states warn without blocking.

Official/source-linked ClawHub metadata remains a trust signal in package records and UI, but it is not a bypass for the release security gate. For example, `@openclaw/kitchen-sink` is treated as an official/source-linked plugin when ClawHub reports that metadata, but install policy still follows the exact release trust response.

## What Changed

- Shared ClawHub release trust gate: plugin installs and skill archive installs now use the same disposition model for clean, review-recommended, review-required, blocked, stale, and unavailable trust data.
- Plugin installs and updates: preserve the existing plugin behavior from the original trust PR, including typed acknowledgement for risky releases and install-record trust metadata.
- Skill installs and updates: ClawHub archive-backed skills check the selected `slug@version` verdict before archive download; owner-qualified skills validate the returned publisher and show `@owner/slug` in warnings and typed acknowledgements.
- Skill CLI: `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update` now accept `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` for reviewed non-interactive automation.
- GitHub-backed ClawHub skills: continue to use the ClawHub install resolver as the policy boundary because the skill security-verdict API is version-based, not commit-resolution based.
- Docs: ClawHub and skills CLI docs now describe the trust gate and acknowledgement flag for both plugins and skills.

## Proof

Reviewer-checkable behavior from the local ClawHub fixture command transcript regenerated at `09144b4e8cdaa57225b6f1339930806f6882a761`:

| Scenario | Command Surface | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| malicious / blocked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install |
| suspicious plugin release without acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `1`, prints `REVIEW REQUIRED` |
| suspicious plugin release with acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package> --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible |
| clean community plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `0` |
| clean official/source-linked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `0` |
| malicious / blocked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install |
| suspicious skill release without acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `1`, prints `REVIEW REQUIRED` |
| suspicious skill release with acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible |
| clean community skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `0` |
| clean official/source-linked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `0` |

The blocked plugin transcript proves the field alignment in the terminal renderer:

```text
╭─ BLOCKED - ClawHub flagged this release as malicious ──────────────────────────╮
│ • Security scan   malicious                                                    │
│ • Moderation      quarantined                                                  │
│ • Finding         manual_moderation                                            │
│ • Scanner         malicious behavior detected                                  │
│ • Finding         Download disabled by ClawHub for this release                │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Links:
  Plugin           http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink
  Security scan    http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink/security/clawscan
  Static analysis  http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink/security/static-analysis
  VirusTotal       http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink/security/virustotal
```

The skill transcript also verifies owner-qualified warning links like `/community/kitchen-sink`, so risky owner-qualified releases are not acknowledged as a bare slug.

## Screenshots

Fresh larger terminal screenshots were regenerated locally for the malicious/suspicious plugin and skill states, but they are not embedded here yet because GitHub image upload was blocked in this environment: `gh-image` has no readable GitHub web upload session or `GH_SESSION_TOKEN`, Chrome/Safari are signed out, and Dia's file-picker flow switched away from the GitHub tab. The command transcript above is the current reviewer-visible proof until the screenshots can be uploaded through a stable GitHub web session.

## Verification

Focused local checks on `09144b4e8cdaa57225b6f1339930806f6882a761`:

- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/clawhub-install-trust.ts src/infra/clawhub.ts src/plugins/clawhub.ts src/plugins/update.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/cli/clawhub-risk-acknowledgement.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/clawhub-install-trust.ts src/infra/clawhub.ts src/plugins/clawhub.ts src/plugins/update.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/cli/clawhub-risk-acknowledgement.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/cli/clawhub-risk-acknowledgement.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts` passed: 3 shards, 400 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugins.install.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugins.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-records.test.ts src/plugins/installs.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/codex-runtime-plugin-install.test.ts` passed: 6 shards, 480 tests.
- `node .artifacts/clawhub-install-trust/run-proof.cjs` passed and regenerated the ten-scenario plugin/skill trust command transcript at `09144b4e8cdaa57225b6f1339930806f6882a761`.

Not run locally: full `pnpm check` / full build. The PR CI is running those broader gates on the pushed head.

target.title: fix(gateway): throttle rapid process restarts before sidecar startup
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** After upgrading to v2026.4.24+, the gateway enters an infinite restart loop on Ubuntu 24 npm installs when the Linux OOM killer terminates the process during sidecar startup.
- **Why it matters:** Every npm/systemd-managed Linux gateway install is exposed — the external restarter (npm, systemd) brings the process back immediately with no delay, and the same heavy sidecar load triggers OOM again, looping indefinitely. The existing internal throttle (`channel-health-monitor`, `MAX_RESTART_ATTEMPTS`) only protects in-process channel-level crashes and cannot intercept a kernel-level SIGKILL.
- **What changed:** Added `server-startup-throttle.ts` — a process-level sentinel that writes `gateway-startup-throttle.json` to `stateDir` on each startup, detects rapid restarts, and sleeps with exponential backoff (5 s → 10 s → 20 s, max 60 s) before loading sidecars. Counter resets after 2 minutes of stable uptime so normal intentional restarts are not penalised. Integrated at the start of the `sidecarsPromise` chain in `server-startup-post-attach.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** Internal channel-level restart logic, sidecar startup sequence and ordering, `minimalTestGateway` code paths, no config schema or env var changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #76275
- Related #79165 — [Feature] Graduated crash recovery ladder (this PR implements the first rung)
- Related #77718 — Null workspace field crash loop (same restart-loop pattern, different trigger)
- Related #78699 — Gateway races launchd KeepAlive port conflict (macOS variant of rapid external restart)
- Related #78791 — Feishu/MiniMax timeout triggers restart (throttle helps; channel-level fix still needed)
- Related #78910 — Discord WS 1006 rapid disconnect loop (channel-level; same external-restart pattern)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Gateway restart loop after OOM kill on Ubuntu 24 npm install post-v2026.4.24.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux (Ubuntu 22.04, Node 24.5.0). Live OOM loop on the reporter's VirtualBox not reproduced; throttle behaviour verified by running the real module directly with `node`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx/esm` to load `server-startup-throttle.ts` directly and simulated 5 rapid sequential starts against a real temp stateDir — no mocking, real fs I/O and real `setTimeout` sleep.
- **Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output from `node --import tsx/esm` exercising the real module, 5 rapid starts:

```
--- gateway start attempt 1 ---
[INFO] sentinel rapidCount=1  backoff_applied=false  elapsed=1ms
[INFO] sidecars would begin loading now

--- gateway start attempt 2 ---
[INFO] sentinel rapidCount=2  backoff_applied=false  elapsed=2ms
[INFO] sidecars would begin loading now

--- gateway start attempt 3 ---
[INFO] sentinel rapidCount=3  backoff_applied=false  elapsed=1ms
[INFO] sidecars would begin loading now

--- gateway start attempt 4 ---
[WARN] gateway: rapid restart detected (4 starts within 60s); backing off 5000ms before loading sidecars
[INFO] sentinel rapidCount=4  backoff_applied=true  elapsed=5007ms
[INFO] sidecars would begin loading now

--- gateway start attempt 5 ---
[WARN] gateway: rapid restart detected (5 starts within 60s); backing off 10000ms before loading sidecars
[INFO] sentinel rapidCount=5  backoff_applied=true  elapsed=10008ms
[INFO] sidecars would begin loading now
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Starts 1–3 pass through with <5 ms overhead. Start 4 triggers the warn log and a measured 5007 ms backoff. Start 5 doubles to 10008 ms. The sentinel file on disk (`gateway-startup-throttle.json`) correctly tracks `rapidCount` across calls. No exception thrown; degradation is graceful.
- **What was not tested:** Full gateway boot with live sidecar OOM on Ubuntu 24 npm/systemd — requires the reporter's diagnostics export to identify the exact OOM-triggering sidecar.
- **Before evidence:** N/A (live OOM loop reproduction requires the reporter's VirtualBox environment).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** Commit `cba92f893d` (`fix(gateway): await startup sidecars by default`) changed the sidecar startup condition from `params.awaitSidecars === true` (opt-in, was test-only) to `params.deferSidecars !== true` (always-on by default). Sidecars that previously ran in the background — plugin services, memory backend, session-lock cleanup — now block the startup path. If any triggers OOM, the Linux kernel kills the process mid-startup; the external restarter fires immediately and the loop begins.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No process-level restart throttle existed. All existing guards (`channel-health-monitor` 10/hour, `MAX_RESTART_ATTEMPTS = 10`) operate on in-process channel state and are reset on every fresh process start.
- **Contributing context:** Ubuntu 24 with npm-managed install (no `RestartSec`/`StartLimitInterval` systemd defaults); OOM-heavy sidecar combination (Codex 5.4, memory backend, plugin services) on a memory-constrained VirtualBox VM.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:**
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/gateway/server-startup-throttle.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `applyStartupRestartThrottle` must sleep when `rapidCount > RAPID_THRESHOLD` and must never sleep on the first start or when count is at threshold. `scheduleStartupThrottleClear` must reset the counter and be cancellable.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The sentinel logic is pure (reads/writes a JSON file + sleeps); no gateway server or sidecar fixture needed. All backoff math and edge cases are directly unit-testable.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None — this is a new module.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A — 12 tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

On 4th+ start within a 60-second window, the gateway logs a warning and delays sidecar startup by 5–60 seconds (exponential backoff). After 2 minutes of stable uptime the counter silently resets. No config change, no new env var, no change to normal single-start or widely-spaced restart behaviour.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[OOM kill] → [npm/systemd restarts immediately] → [sidecars start] → [OOM kill] → loop

After:
[OOM kill] → [npm/systemd restarts] → [sentinel: rapidCount++]
           → [if count > 3: sleep 5s–60s] → [sidecars start]
           → [stable 2 min] → [sentinel: rapidCount reset]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No (reads/writes one JSON file in existing `stateDir`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24 (per issue reporter); dev verified on Linux
- Runtime/container: npm-managed gateway process
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex 5.4 (per reporter)
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: default, no custom stateDir override

### Steps

1. Install gateway via npm on Ubuntu 24 with limited RAM
2. Start gateway — OOM kill occurs during sidecar startup
3. npm/systemd restarts the process immediately
4. Before fix: loop repeats indefinitely
5. After fix: on 4th restart within 60 s, gateway logs throttle warning and sleeps before retrying sidecars

### Expected

- Gateway backs off and allows system memory to recover between restart attempts

### Actual (before fix)

- Gateway restarts immediately each time, triggering the same OOM condition in a tight loop

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Live terminal output captured above (5 rapid starts, real fs I/O, real sleep timings). 32/32 tests pass; `pnpm check:changed` all lanes green.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Threshold boundary (no sleep at count = 3, sleep at count = 4); exponential doubling; 60 s cap; window reset after 61 s; corrupt/missing sentinel handled gracefully; clear timer cancellable; post-attach integration point confirmed in `server-startup-post-attach.ts` diff.
- **Edge cases checked:** `stateDir` does not exist (best-effort write silently ignored); sentinel JSON is corrupt (treated as first start); `rapidCount` overflows to very large value (capped at `BACKOFF_MAX_MS`).
- **What I did not verify:** Live OOM loop reproduction on a memory-constrained Ubuntu 24 VirtualBox npm install — this requires the reporter's exact environment or a matching Crabbox/Testbox scenario.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** False-positive throttle during intentional rapid restarts (e.g. dev hot-reload cycles).
  - **Mitigation:** Threshold is 3 starts in 60 s before any backoff fires; max backoff is 60 s; counter resets after 2 min stable uptime. Development restarts are rarely faster than 3 within a minute.
- **Risk:** `stateDir` write failure silently ignored — no throttle applied if sentinel cannot be persisted.
  - **Mitigation:** Safe degradation — failure to write means the gateway starts normally, no crash. Throttle is a best-effort protective layer, not a hard gate.

target.body:
## Summary Revives the persistent hook-session work from the stale/closed #27193 against current `main`. Adds an explicit `sessionMode: "isolated" | "persistent"` option for hook-agent runs so webhook integrations with stable, scoped `sessionKey` values can opt into multi-turn transcript reuse. Fixes #11665. ## Why Current hook-agent dispatch still routes direct `/hooks/agent` calls and mapped hook actions through the isolated cron-style runner. Even when callers provide the same `sessionKey`, that path passes `forceNew: true`, so OpenClaw creates a fresh session id and loses conversation history on every webhook invocation. Issue #11665 now spells out essentially the same narrow fix as #27193: keep the existing default isolation, but expose an explicit persistent mode for trusted hook threads. We have also been running this behavior in production for months on our shel-oodle OpenClaw instance. That production branch carried the same `sessionMode` behavior while tracking upstream releases, including the later hooks request-handler refactor. ## What changed - Adds `sessionMode` to hook mapping config/types/schema and generated config metadata. - Accepts `sessionMode` on direct `/hooks/agent` payloads. - Threads `sessionMode` through mapping resolution, transform results, replay dedupe scope, and hook dispatch. - Changes `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn` so only explicit `sessionMode: "persistent"` hook-agent runs avoid `forceNew` and can reuse a fresh stored session id. - Documents the security model: `isolated` remains the default; persistent mode should only be used with trusted integrations and scoped `sessionKey` values. Existing request `sessionKey` and prefix policy checks are unchanged. ## Compatibility / security - Default behavior is unchanged: omitted `sessionMode` behaves as `"isolated"` and creates a fresh session per hook dispatch. - Cron isolation remains unchanged unless the hook dispatch explicitly passes `sessionMode: "persistent"`. - Existing `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey` and `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes` checks still gate externally supplied or templated session keys. - Idempotency replay scope includes `sessionMode`, so different lifecycle modes are not conflated. ## Variance from #11665 spec None known. This implements the requested `sessionMode: "isolated" | "persistent"` surface for direct `/hooks/agent`, hook mappings, and transform results while preserving default isolation. ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Hook-agent dispatch now supports explicit persistent hook sessions while preserving isolated default behavior. - **Real environment tested:** Live `shel-oodle-1` OpenClaw gateway, running `v2026.6.1` from this PR branch at `e4bce7737e128e72655a4c388a4ebed66e3b2053`. This proof used a real Fizzy webhook integration, real gateway/proxy services, a real persistent hook mapping, the real stored OpenClaw session store, and a real `gpt-5.5` Codex harness run. Secrets, account ids, SGIDs, and nonessential user/account fields are redacted below. - **Live services during proof:** ```console openclaw-gateway.service active (running) fizzy-webhook.service active (running) cloudflared.service active (running) Gateway version: OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (e4bce77) ``` - **Live webhook delivery proof:** A real Fizzy Playground card (`#1217`) generated real webhook events. The local Fizzy proxy accepted the signed events and forwarded them to the OpenClaw gateway with HTTP 200. ```console Jun 05 01:59:11 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T05:59:11.836Z] info: Fizzy webhook: card_published Jun 05 01:59:11 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T05:59:11.874Z] info: Forwarded to gateway: 200 Jun 05 02:00:10 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:10.288Z] info: Fizzy webhook: comment_created Jun 05 02:00:10 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:10.436Z] info: Forwarded to gateway: 200 Jun 05 02:00:35 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:35.329Z] info: Fizzy webhook: comment_created Jun 05 02:00:35 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:35.482Z] info: Forwarded to gateway: 200 Jun 05 02:00:50 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:50.276Z] info: Fizzy webhook: comment_created Jun 05 02:00:50 shel-oodle node[25078]: [2026-06-05T06:00:50.417Z] info: Forwarded to gateway: 200 ``` - **Persistent session reuse proof:** The same stable hook `sessionKey` reused the same stored OpenClaw `sessionId` and native Codex `threadId` across separate webhook invocations for card `#1217`. ```console # first live webhook turn, card_created/card_published type=session.started ts=2026-06-05T05:59:15.128Z sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 threadId=019e965d-439c-7821-93c5-73f4e521e2ee provider=openai modelId=gpt-5.5 # later live webhook turn, user comment/@mention type=session.started ts=2026-06-05T06:00:36.552Z sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 threadId=019e965d-439c-7821-93c5-73f4e521e2ee provider=openai modelId=gpt-5.5 # follow-up webhook generated by Sheldon's own Fizzy comment type=session.started ts=2026-06-05T06:00:53.300Z sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 threadId=019e965d-439c-7821-93c5-73f4e521e2ee provider=openai modelId=gpt-5.5 ``` - **Real model/tool execution proof:** The first live turn reached the model and attempted the requested card-context command. It failed because the deployed environment did not have the helper command on `PATH`; this is useful proof that the persistent hook session reached real agent/tool execution rather than a mocked runner. ```console type=tool.call ts=2026-06-05T05:59:25.483Z sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 provider=openai modelId=gpt-5.5 command=/bin/bash -lc 'fizzy-context 1217' type=tool.result ts=2026-06-05T05:59:25.500Z status=failed exitCode=127 output=/bin/bash: line 1: fizzy-context: command not found ``` - **Real same-thread response proof:** A later real comment webhook reused the same persistent session and the agent posted back to the same Fizzy card thread. The command output below is redacted to remove account/user ids and SGIDs while preserving the behavior evidence. ```console type=tool.call ts=2026-06-05T06:00:50.086Z sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 provider=openai modelId=gpt-5.5 command=fizzy comment create --card 1217 --body '

[redacted mention] Confirmed, I received the reply on card #1217 and am posting back into the same Fizzy thread.

' type=tool.result ts=2026-06-05T06:00:50.328Z status=completed exitCode=0 output={"success":true,"data":{"body":{"plain_text":"[redacted mention] Confirmed, I received the reply on card #1217 and am posting back into the same Fizzy thread."},"card":{"url":"https://app.fizzy.do/[redacted]/cards/1217"},"creator":{"name":"Sheldon"},"url":"https://app.fizzy.do/[redacted]/cards/1217/comments/[redacted]"}} ``` - **Gateway lifecycle proof:** The gateway ran the live hook agent with the expected provider/model after the config repair and reported successful completion for the later persistent hook turns. ```console Jun 05 02:00:13 shel-oodle node[554078]: [diagnostic] lane wait exceeded: lane=session:agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 waitedMs=3223 queueAhead=0 activeAhead=1 activeNow=0 queueBehind=0 Jun 05 02:00:13 shel-oodle node[554078]: [agent/embedded] selected agent harness: provider=openai model=gpt-5.5 harness=codex requested=- runtimeOverride=- Jun 05 02:00:14 shel-oodle node[554078]: [diagnostic] message processed: channel=cron chatId=unknown messageId=unknown sessionId=b99c7c98-2271-4af9-96f3-8f8a1bc4113a sessionKey=agent:fizzy-orchestrator:hook:fizzy:card-1217 outcome=error duration=59941ms error="fizzy-context 1217 failed" Jun 05 02:00:17 shel-oodle node[554078]: [hooks] hook agent run completed without announcement Jun 05 02:00:52 shel-oodle node[554078]: [agent/embedded] selected agent harness: provider=openai model=gpt-5.5 harness=codex requested=- runtimeOverride=- Jun 05 02:00:53 shel-oodle node[554078]: [hooks] hook agent run completed without announcement Jun 05 02:00:55 shel-oodle node[554078]: [hooks] hook agent run completed without announcement ``` - **Default isolated proof:** The live proof above covers explicit persistent hook reuse in a real setup. Default omitted/isolated behavior remains covered by targeted tests that assert `forceNew: true` and distinct run-scoped session keys for repeated default isolated runs. ```console stdout | ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > uses a run-scoped key for embedded isolated cron execution [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-1 source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle [diagnostic] message processed: channel=cron chatId=unknown messageId=unknown sessionId=isolated-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-1 outcome=completed duration=244ms stdout | ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > keeps embedded isolated cron prompt-cache affinity stable across run sessions [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-a sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-a source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-b sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-b source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle ✓ |cron| ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > uses a run-scoped key for embedded isolated cron execution 476ms ✓ |cron| ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > keeps embedded isolated cron prompt-cache affinity stable across run sessions 137ms ``` - **Observed result after fix:** Persistent hook mode reused the same real stored session and native Codex thread across multiple real webhook deliveries, and a later hook turn posted a real same-card Fizzy comment. Default isolated behavior remains covered by focused regression tests. ## Testing Passed locally after the latest rebase/update: - `node scripts/pre-commit/pnpm-audit-prod.mjs --audit-level=high && pnpm config:schema:check && pnpm config:docs:check && pnpm test src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts && pnpm build` - `pnpm test src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/session.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts` - `pnpm test src/config/config-misc.test.ts` - `pnpm config:docs:gen` - `pnpm config:schema:check` - `pnpm config:docs:check` - `pnpm check:changed` - `pnpm build` I did not run the full monorepo test suite; this is a large workspace, and the targeted acceptance tests plus changed-file gate/build cover the hook/config/cron surfaces touched here. ## Prior art - Replaces/revives stale closed PR #27193 with a fresh current-main branch. - Related issue: #11665.

target.title: fix: macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression
target.body:
## Summary

Found one macOS-only Parallels smoke harness regression. The new guest script writer creates a root-owned `700` script, then the macOS lane executes it as the desktop user.

## What ClawSweeper Is Fixing

- **Low: macOS update script is not readable by the user that executes it** (reliability)
  - File: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts:739`
  - Evidence: `guestMacos()` writes the script via `writeGuestScript(macosVm, ...)`, then runs it through `prlctl exec ... --current-user ... /bin/bash <scriptPath>` or the sudo desktop-user fallback at lines 451-453. But `writeGuestScript()` writes with plain `prlctl exec <vm> /usr/bin/tee <scriptPath>` and then `chmod 700 <scriptPath>` at lines 730-739. The Parallels docs describe `--current-user` as logging in as the current guest OS user, so the write path and execute path are different identities for the macOS lane. ([docs.parallels.com](https://docs.parallels.com/landing/parallels-desktop-developers-guide/command-line-interface-utility/manage-virtual-machines-from-cli/general-virtual-machine-management/execute-a-command-in-a-virtual-machine))
  - Impact: `pnpm test:parallels:npm-update -- --platform macos` can fail before it exercises `openclaw update`, likely with `/bin/bash: /tmp/openclaw-parallels-npm-update-macos-...sh: Permission denied`. That breaks the intended release/update smoke coverage on current `main`; no later commit in the touched files fixes it.
  - Suggested fix: for macOS, write/chmod the temp script through the same resolved transport used to execute it, or explicitly `chown` it to the resolved desktop user and keep permissions non-world-readable because the script embeds provider credentials. Keep the Linux root-owned path separate.
  - Confidence: high

## Expected Repair Surface

- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`

## Source And Review Context

- ClawSweeper report: https://github.com/openclaw/clawsweeper/blob/main/records/openclaw-openclaw/commits/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066.md
- Commit under review: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066
- Latest main at intake: e311ffdcb94e760796a0a8a2c7e58fb8223678bf
- Original commit author: Peter Steinberger
- GitHub author: @steipete
- Highest severity: low
- Review confidence: high

- Diff: `231e5c618f8ae700d51d080e4182a57588da888b..af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066`
- Changed files: `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts`, `scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`
- Code read: full changed files, `scripts/AGENTS.md`, Parallels smoke skill, adjacent guest transport helpers, macOS/Linux smoke helpers, package script entrypoint
- Dependencies/web: official Parallels `prlctl exec` docs; no package dependency changes
- Commands: `pnpm docs:list`, `git show`, `git diff`, `git diff --check`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm exec tsx scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts --help`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`

## Expected validation

- `pnpm check:changed`

ClawSweeper already ran:
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts --help`: passed after installing deps
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-scripts.ts scripts/e2e/parallels/npm-update-smoke.ts`: passed
- `git diff --check ...`: passed

Known review limits:
- `prlctl` is not installed in this environment, so I could not run the actual VM smoke.

## ClawSweeper Guardrails

- Re-check the finding against latest `main` before changing code.
- Keep the patch to the narrowest behavior change and matching regression coverage.
- Do not merge automatically; this PR stays for maintainer review.

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: clawsweeper-commit-openclaw-openclaw-af5a1fbddb14
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Detected by ClawSweeper commit review for af5a1fbddb14a1f1ef2f6e9f3487f108d54ce066.; Original commit author: Peter Steinberger.
- Validation: pnpm check:changed

target.title: feat(whatsapp): add dmRequireMention for DM trigger gating
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `dmRequireMention` config option to WhatsApp channel (and base `ExtensionChannelConfig`) that gates DM replies behind mention-pattern matching
- Reuses existing `mentionPatterns` + `resolveMentionGating` infrastructure — no new pattern config needed
- When enabled, authorized DM senders can chat without triggering a reply on every message; only messages matching a mention pattern trigger the bot

Closes #61607

## Changes

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `src/config/types.channels.ts` | Add `dmRequireMention` to `ExtensionChannelConfig` |
| `src/config/types.whatsapp.ts` | Add `dmRequireMention` to `WhatsAppSharedConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.ts` | Add Zod validation for `dmRequireMention` |
| `extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.ts` | Thread `dmRequireMention` through `ResolvedWhatsAppAccount` |
| `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/on-message.ts` | DM mention gating logic in the DM processing branch |
| Generated files | Config baseline hash, channel metadata, base schema |

## Example config

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "whatsapp": {
      "dmRequireMention": true
    }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      {
        "id": "main",
        "groupChat": {
          "mentionPatterns": ["@openclaw"]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Test plan

- [x] TypeScript type check passes (no new errors)
- [x] `mention-gating.test.ts` passes (core gating logic)
- [x] `accounts.test.ts` passes (account config resolution)
- [x] `config-accessors.test.ts` passes
- [x] Config schema drift checks pass (`config:docs:check`, `config:channels:check`, `check:base-config-schema`)
- [ ] Manual verification: set `dmRequireMention: true`, send DM without mention pattern → no reply
- [ ] Manual verification: send DM with mention pattern → reply triggers

> **Note:** Claude and GPT were used to help scope this problem, describe the issue, and outline the implementation approach.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: [codex] prefer terminal reply fields in CLI JSONL parser
target.body:
﻿﻿## Summary

- Problem: the CLI JSONL parser only special-cases Claude `type:"result"` events and otherwise falls back to generic text collection, so OpenAI/Codex-style fallback runs can lose terminal reply semantics.
- Why it matters: in channel-visible fallback paths, commentary/intermediate text can be surfaced instead of the actual final answer even when the JSONL stream already contains `phase:"final_answer"` and `task_complete.last_agent_message`.
- What changed: `parseCliJsonl()` now prefers `task_complete.last_agent_message`, then assistant messages tagged `phase:"final_answer"`, and skips `commentary`-phase generic item text.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Claude CLI handling is unchanged, no dispatch/channel code changed, and no provider failover logic changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59918
- Related #25592
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: `parseCliJsonl()` was written around Claude `type:"result"` lines plus a generic `parsed.item.text` fallback, but it did not understand newer OpenAI/Codex-style JSONL terminal fields.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no parser preference for `task_complete.last_agent_message` or `phase:"final_answer"`, and no guard against treating `commentary` item text as the outbound reply.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): the current behavior is discussed in #59918 as a narrower parser-path issue adjacent to the broader channel-leak family in #25592.
- Why this regressed now: unknown as a specific regression point; this appears more like a compatibility gap exposed by fallback runs that emit richer JSONL event shapes.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: this fix does not depend on channel dispatching, failover selection, or Claude stream-json parsing.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when JSONL contains commentary, `final_answer`, and `task_complete`, the parser returns the terminal answer; when only `final_answer` is present, it wins over commentary text.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives entirely inside the JSONL parsing function and can be reproduced with synthetic line fixtures without needing a live provider or channel.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Claude result tests in `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts` cover the Claude-specific path and remain unchanged.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- OpenAI/Codex-style CLI fallback runs now prefer terminal reply fields instead of surfacing commentary when both are present in JSONL.
- Commentary-only generic item text is no longer treated as the final reply in this parser path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
CLI JSONL -> generic text collection -> commentary can win -> user sees wrong reply

After:
CLI JSONL -> task_complete / final_answer preferred -> terminal answer wins -> user sees final reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (local contributor checkout)
- Runtime/container: Node.js + pnpm
- Model/provider: parser coverage for OpenAI/Codex-style CLI JSONL output; Claude path rechecked via existing tests
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A for unit verification
- Relevant config (redacted): CLI backend `output: "jsonl"`

### Steps

1. Feed `parseCliJsonl()` a JSONL stream containing commentary events plus a terminal `task_complete.last_agent_message`.
2. Feed `parseCliJsonl()` a JSONL stream containing commentary plus assistant `phase:"final_answer"` content.
3. Run the targeted parser tests.

### Expected

- Terminal answer fields win over commentary.
- Claude JSONL result handling still passes unchanged.

### Actual

- Verified with targeted tests after the patch.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked: existing Claude stream-json tests still pass; `task_complete` outranks `final_answer`; `final_answer` outranks commentary; commentary `item.text` is ignored.
- What you did **not** verify: I did not run a live WhatsApp/Telegram fallback end-to-end against a real CLI provider from this branch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI/Codex-style CLI JSONL output is now parsed so terminal reply records win over commentary or intermediate message text in both the full-buffer parser and streaming parser.

Real environment tested: Windows/PowerShell checkout at PR head `8b90301ef3`; installed OpenAI Codex CLI `codex-cli 0.130.0-alpha.5`; Node `v24.13.1`; OpenClaw parser entry points imported directly from `src/agents/cli-output.ts`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
codex exec --json --ephemeral --ignore-rules --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check "Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_IGNORE_RULES_OK" 1> .artifacts/real-proof-59920/codex-ignore-rules.jsonl 2> .artifacts/real-proof-59920/codex-ignore-rules.stderr.txt

node --import tsx --input-type=module
imports parseCliJsonl() and createCliJsonlStreamingParser() from ./src/agents/cli-output.ts
feeds the saved Codex CLI JSONL transcript through both parser paths

node --import tsx --input-type=module
imports the same parser entry points and replays redacted Codex/OpenAI terminal-field JSONL containing commentary, final_answer, and task_complete records
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ codex exec --json --ephemeral --ignore-rules --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check "Reply exactly: FINAL_PROOF_IGNORE_RULES_OK"

For the bot-cited terminal-field shape, both parser paths returned `TASK_COMPLETE_WINS`; `COMMENTARY_SHOULD_NOT_WIN` and the lower-priority `FINAL_ANSWER_SHOULD_BE_OVERRIDDEN` were not surfaced.

- Risk: a non-Claude JSONL backend could intentionally rely on commentary-phase `item.text` as the only final output.

target.title: Android: lower minSdk to 26
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Current minSdk version was too high, preventing users on older Android devices (API 26-30) from installing the app. Additionally, the UI relied on dynamic color features (Material You) available only in newer Android versions, which would cause crashes or rendering issues on older APIs.
- Why it matters: Lowering the minSdk to 26 (Android 8.0) significantly expands our potential user base while maintaining support for essential modern background limits and security features.
- What changed:
  - Updated build.gradle to set minSdk 26.
  - Added theme color fallbacks for devices that do not support Dynamic Color (API < 31).
  - Refactored NodeForegroundService.kt to include NotificationChannel setup and compliant startForeground logic for API 26+.
  - Added a conditional check for DnsResolver to prevent crashes on Android 8.0-10, with an automatic fallback to a direct UDP resolver (port 53).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes were made to the core business logic or higher-level API features (API 31+); those users will still experience dynamic coloring.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Users on Android 8.0 (Oreo) through Android 11 can now install and run the app.
- On Android 12+, the app continues to use dynamic system colors.
- On Android 8.0-11, the app will use a consistent, predefined brand color palette.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Android 8.0 (API 26) Emulator / Android 14 (API 34) Physical Device
- Runtime/container: Android
- Model/provider: Pixel 2 (Emulator), Pixel 7 (Physical)
- Integration/channel (if any):
- Relevant config (redacted):

### Steps

1. Build and install the app on an Android 8.0 (API 26) device/emulator.
2. Start the Node service and verify that the Foreground Notification appears correctly (validating the NotificationChannel setup).
3. Test network-related features (like gateway discovery) to ensure the DNS fallback works.

### Expected

- The app installs and opens successfully on API 26.

### Actual

- App launches without crash; theme gracefully falls back to static brand colors on API 26.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets [build_output.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26076480/build_output.txt)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: App launch, main navigation, and settings screen on API 26.
- Edge cases checked: Dark mode toggle on older Android versions (ensure values-night also has fallbacks).
- What you did **not** verify: Did not test on every single API level between 26 and 31 (sampled 29).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the minSdk change in build.gradle and roll back OpenClawTheme.kt file.
- Files/config to restore: app/build.gradle, OpenClawTheme.kt.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: UI crashes (Resource Not Found) on older devices if a fallback color is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

None

target.title: fix(skills): list omitted skill names when prompt is truncated
target.body:
## Problem

When many skills are installed (e.g. 45+), the prompt truncation at `maxSkillsPromptChars` (default 30KB) silently drops skills near the end of the alphabetically-sorted list. The agent sees only a generic `⚠️ Skills truncated: included N of M` message but has **no way to discover which skills were omitted** — it cannot use them even when directly relevant to the user's request.

Real-world example: a user sends a `mp.weixin.qq.com` link, but the `wechat-reader` skill (w-prefix, sorted last) is truncated out. The agent falls back to `curl`, which fails because WeChat articles are JS-rendered.

## Solution

When skills are truncated, the note now includes the **names** of omitted skills (capped at 1KB to stay compact) and suggests `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` so the agent can still discover and read them on demand.

**Before:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

**After:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit. Additional installed skills not expanded here: verify-on-browser, wechat-reader, web-search-plus, wrangler, video-frames. If a listed skill name becomes relevant, inspect it with `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` before deciding whether to read its SKILL.md.
```

## Changes

- `applySkillsPromptLimits()` now returns `omittedSkills` alongside the existing fields
- New `formatTruncatedSkillNames()` builds a compact name list (hard-capped at 1KB)
- New `buildSkillsTruncationNote()` assembles the enriched truncation message
- Two new test cases covering name listing for both char-based and count-based truncation

## Tests

All 34 existing + new tests pass (`npx vitest run src/agents/skills`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Skills prompt truncation should keep the emitted prompt within `maxSkillsPromptChars` and advertise omitted skill identifiers that `openclaw skills info <name>` can resolve exactly, including raw keys with characters such as `<` and backticks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.2, branch `fix/skills-truncation-visibility`, current HEAD `db926508c4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rendered 80 synthetic workspace skills through the real `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()` code path with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520` and raw key `skill-00<zero`raw`; then parsed the emitted `<omitted_skill_keys>` payload and resolved the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup. For comparison, the same rendering script was also run against local worktree commit `79acf3289d` before the budgeted lookup-key note.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the local command run.

```text
Before (79acf3289d):
{
  "commit": "before",
  "cap": 520,
  "length": 989,
  "hasOmittedSkillKeys": false,
  "advertisedCount": 0,
  "advertisedFirst": null,
  "hasRawKey": false
}
--- prompt excerpt ---
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 0 of 80 (compact format, descriptions omitted). Omitted: skill-00zeroraw, skill-01, skill-02, skill-03, skill-04, skill-05, skill-06, skill-07, skill-08, skill-09, skill-10, skill-11, skill-12, skill-13, skill-14, skill-15, skill-16, skill-17, skill-18, skill-19, skill-20, skill-21, skill-22, skill-23, skill-24, skill-25, skill-26, skill-27, skill-28, skill-29, skill-30, skill-31, skill-32, skill-33, skill-34, skill-35, skill-36, skill-37, skill-38, skill-39, skill-40, skill-41, skill-42, skill-43, skill-44, skill-45, skill-46, skill-47, skill-48, skill-49, skill-50, skill-51, skill-52, skill-53, skill-54, skill-55, skill-56, skill-57, skill-58, skill-59, skill-60, skill-61, skill-62, skill-63, skill-64, skill-65, skill-66, skill-67, skill-68, skill-69, skill-70, skill-71, skill-72, skill-73, skill-74, skill-75, skill-76, skill-77, skill-78, skill-79. Use `openclaw skills info <name>` to load them on demand. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.

After (db926508c4):
{
  "commit": "after",
  "cap": 520,
  "length": 512,
  "hasOmittedSkillKeys": true,
  "advertisedCount": 21,
  "advertisedFirst": "skill-00<zero`raw",
  "hasRawKey": true,
  "exactLookupFirst": "skill-00<zero`raw"
}
--- prompt excerpt ---
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 0 of 80 (compact format, descriptions omitted). Omitted skill lookup keys for `openclaw skills info <name>` (JSON data; use exact string values): <omitted_skill_keys>["skill-00\u003czero`raw","skill-01","skill-02","skill-03","skill-04","skill-05","skill-06","skill-07","skill-08","skill-09","skill-10","skill-11","skill-12","skill-13","skill-14","skill-15","skill-16","skill-17","skill-18","skill-19","skill-20"]</omitted_skill_keys> (+59 more) Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

Observed result after fix: The current prompt is 512 chars, below the 520 char cap, includes `<omitted_skill_keys>`, preserves the raw advertised key as JSON data, and `formatSkillInfo()` resolves the first advertised key exactly as `skill-00<zero`raw`.

What was not tested: Live model submission was not needed for this prompt-rendering fix; the local proof covers the real prompt builder and CLI exact lookup formatter path.

Local script generated 80 workspace skills with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520`, injected the raw omitted lookup key `skill-00<zero`raw`, and rendered the same prompt path through `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()`. The after case also passes the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup.

Before (`79acf3289d`, pre budgeted lookup-key note):

Use `openclaw skills info <name>` to load them on demand. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

After (`db926508c4`):

```text

Omitted skill lookup keys for `openclaw skills info <name>` (JSON data; use exact string values): <omitted_skill_keys>["skill-00\u003czero`raw","skill-01","skill-02","skill-03","skill-04","skill-05","skill-06","skill-07","skill-08","skill-09","skill-10","skill-11","skill-12","skill-13","skill-14","skill-15","skill-16","skill-17","skill-18","skill-19","skill-20"]</omitted_skill_keys> (+59 more) Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.

target.title: Capture image-generation provider usage metadata
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve Google Gemini image `usageMetadata` in image result metadata when returned
- Preserve OpenAI/GPT Image 2 `usage` in direct and OpenAI-compatible image provider paths
- Preserve factual fal/Flux response metadata fields when returned, without synthesizing token usage

## Live payload validation
- MAI Image 2 generated successfully but returned no usage field, so local JSON helper now omits usage instead of emitting nulls
- Azure GPT Image 2 returned `usage`: input_tokens=19, output_tokens=196, total_tokens=215
- Azure FLUX.2-pro returned `created`, `data`, `request_meta`; no token usage

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Image-generation cost/usage metadata was being dropped. Google Gemini image usageMetadata, OpenAI/GPT Image usage, and provider-returned Flux metadata now flow into image result metadata when the API actually returns them.
- **Real environment tested:** GordieBox Mac Studio, local OpenClaw workspace and Azure image resources. Live payload validation used the configured Azure MAI Image 2, Azure GPT Image 2, and Azure FLUX.2-pro endpoints with real API responses; no secrets are included here.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  npm test -- --run src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/litellm/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/image-generation-provider.test.ts
  CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions
  python3 -m py_compile skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate.py skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_gpt.py skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_flux.py
  python3 -m py_compile repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate.py repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_gpt.py repos/Gordie-OpenClaw/shared/skills/azure-image-gen/scripts/generate_flux.py
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live output from GordieBox/Azure:
  ```text
  Azure GPT Image 2 response usage -> input_tokens=19, output_tokens=196, total_tokens=215
  MAI Image 2 response keys -> data only; usage field absent
  Azure FLUX.2-pro response keys -> created, data, request_meta; usage field absent
  ```
  The patched OpenAI-compatible/direct OpenAI paths preserve the GPT Image 2 usage as `metadata.usage`. MAI helper JSON now omits usage when absent. The Flux path captures returned factual response metadata only.
- **Observed result after fix:** Returned usage is captured for Google/OpenAI-compatible/OpenAI when present, Flux records only actual provider-returned metadata, and MAI/Flux do not invent token counts when their live response omits usage. `CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed after the OpenAI response typing cleanup.
- **What was not tested:** No public FAL live call, because no FAL key is configured in this environment. Google live generation was not repeated for this PR because the known API contract returns `usageMetadata`; the patch is covered with provider tests and prior payload investigation.

## Validation
- `npm test -- --run src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/litellm/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/image-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/image-generation-provider.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- Typechecked Vitest runs for modified provider tests: OpenAI, Google, fal, LiteLLM, OpenAI-compatible helper

Refs #85461
